name: calendar-gws-provider
goal: Add a third calendar provider backed by the gws CLI so users on Google Workspace via gws can use arete pull calendar / availability find / calendar create without configuring the legacy Google Calendar OAuth flow.

userStories.title: Implement gws-calendar.ts provider
userStories.description: Create packages/core/src/integrations/calendar/gws-calendar.ts implementing the full CalendarProvider interface using gwsExec exclusively. Mirror google-calendar.ts shape but use 'gws calendar events list / calendarList list / freebusy query / events insert' commands. Set name: 'gws-calendar'. Read first: types.ts, google-calendar.ts (L43-52, L232-259, L429-585), gws/client.ts, gws/gmail.ts, gws/LEARNINGS.md. Mitigations: R3 (isAvailable wraps all errors → returns false; 4 unit tests cover GwsNotInstalledError, GwsAuthError, GwsTimeoutError, generic Error), R7 (extract only getTodayRange + getUpcomingRange to calendar/date-helpers.ts; do NOT share mapGoogleEvent / resolveCalendarNames / fetchCalendarList), R8 (.js extensions; accept optional deps?: { exec?: GwsDeps['exec'] } and thread through every gwsExec call).
acceptanceCriteria: File at packages/core/src/integrations/calendar/gws-calendar.ts exports getGwsCalendarProvider(deps?): CalendarProvider
New packages/core/src/integrations/calendar/date-helpers.ts exports getTodayRange and getUpcomingRange; google-calendar.ts updated to import from it (no behavior change)
Returned provider has name === 'gws-calendar'
getTodayEvents/getUpcomingEvents call gwsExec('calendar', 'events list', { calendarId, timeMin, timeMax, singleEvents:true, orderBy:'startTime', maxResults:250 }) with pageToken pagination
Mapper produces CalendarEvent with Date instances for startTime/endTime; isAllDay === true for items with start.date only
Mapper preserves organizer.self === true and recurringEventId from fixture data
getFreeBusy calls gwsExec('calendar', 'freebusy query', { timeMin, timeMax, items }) and returns FreeBusyResult with Date instances
createEvent calls gwsExec('calendar', 'events insert', {...}) and returns { id, htmlLink, summary, start, end }

isAvailable() runs gws calendar events list maxResults:1 round-trip; returns true on success; returns false (no throw) for GwsNotInstalledError, GwsAuthError, GwsTimeoutError, generic Error — 4 separate unit tests

userStories.title: Wire provider factory
userStories.description: Update packages/core/src/integrations/calendar/index.ts getCalendarProvider to handle provider === 'gws'. Maintain existing branches for 'google', 'ical-buddy', 'macos' exactly. Add comment 'configure writes gws — keep in sync'. Read first: calendar/index.ts, packages/core/test/integrations/calendar/factory.test.ts (L87 'google' case is the template). Mitigation: this is the canonical 2026-02-11 producer-consumer alignment check — factory regression test must use the exact value written by configure.
acceptanceCriteria: getCalendarProvider({ integrations: { calendar: { provider: 'gws' } } }, storage, root) returns provider with name === 'gws-calendar'
Factory regression test added in factory.test.ts mirroring the 'google' case at L87
Existing factory tests for 'google', 'ical-buddy', 'macos', null provider all still pass

userStories.title: Add gws-calendar registry entry
userStories.description: Add 'gws-calendar' entry to packages/core/src/integrations/registry.ts: displayName: 'Google Calendar (gws)', description: 'Google Calendar via gws CLI (no OAuth required)', implements: ['calendar'], auth: { type: 'none' }, status: 'available'. Place adjacent to existing google-calendar entry.
acceptanceCriteria: INTEGRATIONS['gws-calendar'] is defined with the exact shape above
arete integration list shows gws-calendar in the available integrations list (verified by snapshot or test assertion)

userStories.title: Wire integration list alias mapping
userStories.description: Update packages/core/src/services/integrations.ts:122-137 calendar provider alias map. Add a third branch: when calendarProvider === 'gws' and calendarStatus is set, set configured['gws-calendar'] = calendarStatus as IntegrationStatus. Read first: services/integrations.ts:108-140, find the existing alias-mapping test in packages/core/test/services/. Mitigation R4-extended: this is the consumer-side counterpart to task-2's factory wiring; the 2026-02-11 incident pattern requires the test to use the exact value written by configure.
acceptanceCriteria: With arete.yaml containing integrations.calendar.{provider:'gws',status:'active'}, IntegrationService.list() includes 'gws-calendar': 'active' in configured
Regression test added mirroring existing 'google' and 'macos' alias tests in the same file
Existing alias tests for 'google' and 'macos'/'ical-buddy' still pass

userStories.title: Configure UX — interactive picker + cleanup + smoke test
userStories.description: Convert the name === 'calendar' branch in packages/cli/src/commands/integration.ts:98-136 from non-interactive (writes provider:'macos' unconditionally) to interactive. (1) Inquirer select with three labeled choices: 'macOS (ical-buddy)', 'Google Calendar (OAuth)', 'Google Workspace (gws)' — match onboard.ts runIntegrationPhase pageSize:12. (2) Add --provider <gws|google|macos> non-interactive flag for testability. (3) Selecting gws runs smoke test gws calendar events list maxResults:1 with 5s timeout BEFORE writing config; on GwsAuthError/timeout abort with 'Run gws auth login first, then re-run configure'. (4) After provider chosen, prompt (default Y) to clear losers' config: gws→delete integrations.google-calendar block + .arete/secrets/google-calendar.json via storage.delete; google→delete integrations.calendar.provider:gws; macos→both. (5) Preserve --calendars/--all flags for macos path. Read first: integration.ts:80-140, onboard.ts runIntegrationPhase, cli/commands/LEARNINGS.md (Non-interactive flags + Integration phase pattern), gws/client.ts. Mitigations: R2 (use storage.delete NOT fs), R6 (smoke test before write; do not write on failure), Memory: established CLI patterns over bare minimum.
acceptanceCriteria: arete integration configure calendar (no flags, TTY) presents inquirer select with exactly three labeled choices in order: 'macOS (ical-buddy)', 'Google Calendar (OAuth)', 'Google Workspace (gws)'; pageSize 12

arete integration configure calendar --provider gws --json runs non-interactively, runs smoke test, writes integrations.calendar.provider:gws if smoke passes, prints { success:true, integration:'calendar', provider:'gws' }

Smoke-test failure unit test in cli/test/commands/integration.test.ts: inject gwsExec that throws GwsAuthError. Assert (a) command exits non-zero, (b) arete.yaml is unchanged, (c) stderr contains literal 'gws auth login'

Transition test 1 (google→gws): pre-state has integrations.google-calendar populated AND .arete/secrets/google-calendar.json in mock storage. Run --provider gws (confirm seeded Y). Assert: integrations.google-calendar block removed; storage.delete('.arete/secrets/google-calendar.json') called; integrations.calendar.provider==='gws'
Transition test 2 (gws→macos): pre-state integrations.calendar.provider:gws. Run --provider macos. Assert: integrations.calendar.provider==='macos'; no provider:gws lingers
Transition test 3 (gws→google): pre-state integrations.calendar.provider:gws. Run --provider google. Assert: integrations.calendar block cleared; integrations.google-calendar configured
Cleanup-decline test: same gws→macos transition with confirm seeded N — assert prior config is preserved (no cleanup)

Backward-compat: existing test 'configures calendar integration with default macos provider' (integration.test.ts:25-54) is updated to pass --provider macos explicitly per CLI LEARNINGS Non-interactive flags pattern
Existing --calendars / --all flags work when paired with --provider macos OR alone (auto-implies macos)
--provider accepts only gws|google|macos; invalid value exits non-zero with error listing valid options

userStories.title: Update CLI error messages for multi-provider
userStories.description: Three CLI files hardcode 'Google Calendar' where gws now also satisfies the requirement. (1) availability.ts:135,139: replace 'Availability requires Google Calendar. Run: arete integration configure google-calendar' with 'Availability requires a calendar provider supporting free/busy. Run: arete integration configure calendar'. (2) calendar.ts:317,321: replace 'Event creation requires Google Calendar. Run: arete integration configure google-calendar' with 'Event creation requires a calendar provider supporting event creation. Run: arete integration configure calendar'. (3) pull.ts:422-431: extend provider.name switch to add 'gws-calendar' case: errorMsg='Google Workspace not authenticated', helpMsg='Run: gws auth login'.
acceptanceCriteria: rg 'requires Google Calendar' packages/cli/src/ returns zero matches

Unit test in cli/test/commands/availability.test.ts (new or extend): with no calendar provider returning getFreeBusy, JSON output contains error: 'Availability requires a calendar provider supporting free/busy' and message: 'Run: arete integration configure calendar'

Unit test in cli/test/commands/calendar.test.ts (new or extend): with no calendar provider returning createEvent, JSON output contains error: 'Event creation requires a calendar provider supporting event creation'

Unit test in cli/test/commands/pull.test.ts (extend): for a provider stub with name==='gws-calendar' and isAvailable()→false, JSON output contains error: 'Google Workspace not authenticated' and message: 'Run: gws auth login'
rg 'provider\.name\s*===\s*' packages/cli/src/ shows gws-calendar case is handled in pull.ts:422-431 alongside ical-buddy and google-calendar
Generic else branch in pull.ts:428-431 reachable only for truly unknown provider names

userStories.title: Tests — gws-calendar provider + fixture replacement
userStories.description: Create packages/core/test/integrations/calendar/gws-calendar.test.ts mirroring google-calendar.test.ts structure. Mock gwsExec via DI (NOT real child_process). CRITICAL R1: existing packages/core/test/integrations/gws/fixtures/calendar-events.json is a hand-crafted 10-line stub referenced by zero tests — REPLACE before writing the mapper. Capture real fixtures: calendar-events-list.json (≥1 all-day, ≥1 recurring, ≥1 organizer.self===true per R5), calendar-list.json, calendar-freebusy.json, calendar-event-insert.json. Read first: google-calendar.test.ts (mirror exactly), gws/gmail.test.ts (DI pattern), the orphan fixture to replace.
acceptanceCriteria: Orphan packages/core/test/integrations/gws/fixtures/calendar-events.json is DELETED; new calendar-events-list.json captured from real gws CLI per Testing Strategy §4.2 capture protocol
Sanity check: jq '[.items[] | select(.start.date)] | length' on calendar-events-list.json returns ≥1 (all-day)
Sanity check: jq '[.items[] | select(.recurringEventId)] | length' returns ≥1
Sanity check: jq '[.items[] | select(.organizer.self == true)] | length' returns ≥1
Three additional fixtures captured from real gws: calendar-list.json, calendar-freebusy.json, calendar-event-insert.json

≥10 unit tests in gws-calendar.test.ts: (a) getTodayEvents returns mapped events with Date instances, (b) getUpcomingEvents 7-day window, (c) mapper preserves organizer.self===true, (d) mapper preserves recurringEventId, (e) mapper sets isAllDay correctly for start.date-only events, (f) getFreeBusy returns FreeBusyResult with Date instances and per-email accessible flags, (g) createEvent returns { id, htmlLink, summary, start, end }, (h) isAvailable false on GwsNotInstalledError, (i) isAvailable false on GwsAuthError, (j) isAvailable false on GwsTimeoutError, (k) isAvailable false on generic Error
Factory regression test added in factory.test.ts mirroring 'google' case at L87 (per task-2)
IntegrationService.list alias-mapping regression test added in integrations.test.ts L328 describe block (per task-4)
Tests inject gwsExec via DI (no real child_process calls in tests; verify with rg 'execFile|spawn' packages/core/test/integrations/calendar/gws-calendar.test.ts returns zero)
npm test -w @arete/core passes; npm test -w @arete/cli passes

userStories.title: Skill + LEARNINGS docs
userStories.description: Update packages/runtime/skills/calendar/SKILL.md (around L35: list three providers; L38-41: update no-calendar hint; L82-86: add auth_expired (gws) row pointing to 'gws auth login'). Update packages/core/src/integrations/gws/LEARNINGS.md Verified command paths table (L14-27): add 4 rows for events list, calendarList list, freebusy query, events insert with exact --params shapes used in gws-calendar.ts. Document any response-shape gaps observed during fixture capture in a new 'Calendar Response Shape Notes' section, or note 'no gaps observed'.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md L35 lists three calendar providers (macOS / Google Calendar / Google Workspace)
SKILL.md error table includes auth_expired (gws) row pointing to 'gws auth login'
gws/LEARNINGS.md Verified command paths table includes 4 new calendar rows with real --params JSON
Any response-shape gaps observed during fixture capture are documented (or 'no gaps observed' is noted)
